summary: Excellent audience recording with crisp vocals and rich bass, but a mixed‑bag show – scorching “Stranger” and “Deal” shine while the first‑set lull and weak “Bucket” drag it down.
review: The AUD tape captures the 1983 performance with surprising clarity – punchy vocals, minimal crowd bleed and a full, tight low end. Musically the band delivers high points like a fiery “Stranger”, a blazing “Deal”, a soaring Spanish jam (Space) and a stellar China Cat, yet several listeners cite a sleepy first set and a sub‑par Hell in a Bucket that keep the overall show feeling uneven.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised (near‑perfect AUD), but show assessments range from rave to disappointment, pulling the combined weighted score to a solid mid‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, punchy vocals with very little crowd noise; full, rich bass and balanced instruments. A few reviewers note a warbling/tape‑like artifact from the China Cat segment through the second half.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, tight first set opens hot with a funky “Stranger” but loses momentum after a few slower numbers before reviving on the blazing “Deal”. The second set is uneven – moments of brilliance (China Cat, intense Space jam) are interspersed with warbling tape issues and a lackluster Bucket.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Scorching lead guitar, great tone and intense jamming; highlighted on “Deal” and the Spanish jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Pre‑drums described as "hot"; his bass work shakes the rear‑view mirror and he even blew kisses to the crowd.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards are noticeable, especially a shift toward his signature sound late in the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight rhythm section with solid bass foundation; occasional low energy from the audience noted.
